title: Fixing Invalid Git Tag Name Validation

statement.markdown: Write a function that validates a Git tag name. A valid tag name must not be empty, must be alphanumeric (a-z, A-Z, 0-9), and must not contain any special characters. Your function should return `true` if the tag name is valid, and `false` otherwise. Note that this is not a comprehensive validation, but rather a simplified version for this exercise. You can assume that the input is a string.
statement.inputFormat: A single string representing the tag name to be validated.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the tag name is valid or not.
statement.notes: This problem does not require handling edge cases like invalid characters. It assumes the input will always be a string.

hints.text: Check if the input string is empty
